title: Service entrypoint contract

commands.summary: Final S01 closeout gate rerun after evidence creation

notes: No real client, matter, document, billing, settlement, credential, or secret data was used.

All commands are local validation, parser, test, Claude read-only review, or git whitespace checks; none execute service logic, create runtime routes, invoke Claude runtime review, write review queues, write review assignments, send review notifications, replace human approval, append audit ledgers, write audit events, write databases, write storage, or persist product state.
Pre-closeout npm run rp00:control-plane:validate failed only because S01 evidence files were intentionally not created yet; this command is rerun after evidence creation as the final gate.
An initial Claude CLI attempt returned API Error 529 Overloaded and is recorded only as an invalid partial attempt. It is not counted as the completed review evidence unit.

Claude C00 valid review completed once. Claude rated the S01 implementation GO with no P0/P1/P2 findings. Two P3 notes are informational and explicitly deferred: database/storage no-write is asserted by the declaration-only boundary and prior S01 convention, and the review-context list intentionally includes two strengthening contexts beyond the eight named requirements.

evidence_summary.claude_review_dependency: C00 actual claude-opus-4-8 max read-only review completed once before production_ready closeout; the earlier 529 overload did not produce a valid review and is excluded from exactly_one_valid_review_run.
evidence_summary.human_approval_boundary: Human approval is not replaced or synthesized by this deterministic synthetic contract boundary because no sensitive production mutation, client-facing output, external sharing, finance action, real data operation, runtime execution, audit write, storage write, database write, or product-state write occurs.
evidence_summary.service_entrypoint_contract: S01 defines the Claude review service entrypoint contract, preserves the M00 three canonical runtime entrypoints, requires S20 production_ready input, locks model and read-only review semantics, keeps RP00.P02.M04 open, and hands off to RP00.P02.M04.S02.
